title: Modern Cutout Nightstand
summary: A pair of matching nightstands cut from solid 1x18 boards, each with two drawers and a cutout detail on the side panels standing in for a traditional pull.
description:
This plan builds two identical nightstands from wide 1x18 boards — top, bottom, and sides all cut from the same stock, joined into a simple open case with cleats supporting the drawers and a footer trimming the base. **The side panels carry a modern cutout detail** rather than a flat face, which is the piece's whole design idea and what separates it from this catalog's other plywood-box nightstands.

Build both nightstands together rather than one at a time, since keeping two cases identical is far easier when every cut is made in a matched pair. Each nightstand gets two stacked drawers running on corner-mount slides with a 1/2" side clearance — check that clearance against your actual slide hardware before cutting the drawer boxes, since a mismatch there is what causes a drawer to bind.

The plywood back panel closes each case in, and the drawer faces are cut from the same plywood sheet as the case per the cut layout, keeping the grain and finish consistent across the whole pair.

materials.name: 3/4" MDF (for painting) or A1 hardwood plywood (for staining), 4x8 sheet
Drawer slides, 16 in, 1/2" clearance, corner mount

cutList.material: 3/4" MDF (for painting) or A1 hardwood plywood (for staining), 4x8 sheet
3/4" MDF (for painting) or A1 hardwood plywood (for staining), 4x8 sheet
3/4" MDF (for painting) or A1 hardwood plywood (for staining), 4x8 sheet
cutList.note: Cut per the plywood cut layout to keep grain consistent.

steps.title: Cut the case parts for both nightstands
steps.body: Cut the tops, bottoms, and sides for both nightstands as a matched pair, including the cutout detail on the side panels.
materials: 3/4" MDF (for painting) or A1 hardwood plywood (for staining), 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Build both cases
steps.body: Assemble the tops, bottoms, and sides into two identical cases, then attach the cleats and footers.

steps.title: Attach the backs
steps.body: Nail the plywood back panel to each case.

steps.title: Install the drawer slides
steps.body: Check the 1/2" side clearance on your actual slide hardware, then mount the slides in each case.
materials: Drawer slides, 16 in, 1/2" clearance, corner mount

steps.title: Build the drawers
steps.body: Assemble the four drawer boxes from their sides, backs, and plywood bottoms, then attach the drawer faces cut to the layout.
materials: 3/4" MDF (for painting) or A1 hardwood plywood (for staining), 4x8 sheet

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Fill any gaps with wood filler, sand, and apply your chosen finishing supplies.

unresolvedImages.alt: Pair of modern cutout nightstands with two drawers each
